items.definition_en: A chair is something you sit on.
items.example_en: She put her bag on the chair.

items.definition_en: A table is a piece of furniture with a flat top and legs.
items.example_en: We eat dinner at the table.

items.definition_en: A bookcase is a piece of furniture used to hold books.
items.example_en: The bookcase is full of novels.

items.definition_en: A drawer is a box that slides in and out of furniture.
items.example_en: I keep my socks in the top drawer.

items.definition_en: A bench is a long seat for several people.
items.example_en: He sat on the bench by the window.

items.definition_en: A nightstand is a small table next to a bed.
items.example_en: There is a lamp on my nightstand.

items.definition_en: A dressing table is a table where you get ready and keep cosmetics.
items.example_en: Her hairbrush is on the dressing table.

items.definition_en: A book shelf is a flat part of furniture for holding books.
items.example_en: He arranged the books on the book shelf.

items.definition_en: A footstool is a small seat for resting your feet on.
items.example_en: She put her feet on the footstool.

items.definition_en: A sofabed is a sofa that can be turned into a bed.
items.example_en: We have a sofabed for guests.

items.definition_en: A stool is a seat with no back or arms.
items.example_en: He sat on a stool at the counter.

items.definition_en: A table lamp is a small lamp that sits on a table.
items.example_en: She turned on the table lamp to read.

items.definition_en: A bench seat is a wide seat where several people sit together.
items.example_en: There is a bench seat at the dining table.

items.definition_en: A recliner is a chair that leans back for comfort.
items.example_en: My dad likes to relax in his recliner.

items.definition_en: A rocking chair is a chair that moves back and forth gently.
items.example_en: The grandmother sat in the rocking chair.

items.definition_en: A side table is a small table placed beside a chair or sofa.
items.example_en: I put my drink on the side table.

items.definition_en: A headboard is the part of a bed that sits behind your head.
items.example_en: She leaned against the headboard to read.

items.definition_en: A high chair is a tall chair for a baby to sit in at a table.
items.example_en: The baby sat happily in the high chair.

items.definition_en: An ottoman is a soft seat or footrest with no back or arms.
items.example_en: He rested his feet on the ottoman.

items.definition_en: A bunk bed is two beds, one on top of the other.
items.example_en: The kids sleep in a bunk bed.
